FIRST REPORT OF WATERSPOUT WAS MADE BY THE PUBLIC AT 0113 PM EST, TWO NWS EMPLOYEES WENT TO THE ROOF OF THE WHITE STREET OFFICE AND OBSERVED 14 SPOUTS IN TOTAL ENDING AT 0230 PM EST. AT ONE POINT THERE WERE 5 SPOUTS LIVE.

DELAYED REPORT OF MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS BY AN OFF DUTY WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE DRIVING HOME FROM AN OVERNIGHT SHIFT. THREE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED WITHIN A CUMULUS LINE SOUTH OF THE EASTERN LOWER KEYS. DURATION WAS 30 MINUTES FOR THE MOST ROBUST OF THE THREE WATERSPOUTS, WHILE THE OTHER TWO WERE SHORT LIVED AND LASTED 10 MINUTES OR LESS. THE FUNNEL FOR THE STRONGEST WATERSPOUT WAS SIMILAR TO AN ELEPHANT TRUNK. SPRAY RINGS WERE NOT OBSERVED.

TWO WATERSPOUTS REPORTED FROM TRAINED SPOTTER. BOTH SPOUTS LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES. FIRST SPOUT STARTED AT 140 PM EDT. SPOUT WAS PENCIL THIN AND DARK GREY. SECOND SPOUT, AT 143 PM EDT, WAS MEDIUM IN SIZE AND ALSO DARK GREY. BOTH SPOUTS WERE UNOBSTRUCTED AND EVENTUALLY OVERTAKEN BY RAIN SHAFTS.

DELAYED REPORT. STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR STOCK ISLAND WATERFRONT. DAMAGE TO ROOFS, SHEDS, AND BOAT COVERINGS OCCURRED. NUMEROUS SMALL TREE LIMBS WERE DOWN. PLEASE SEE THE PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT FOR MORE INFORMATION.

A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMEBER OF THE PUBLIC OVER SOCIAL MEDIA NEAR SADDLEBUNCH AND SUGARLOAF KEYS. THE WATERSPOUT LASTED BETWEN 5 TO 7 MINUTES AND THE DIRECTION OF MOVEMENT COULD NOT BE DETERMINED. NO SPRAY RING WAS EVIDENT, BUT IT WAS 5 MILES AWAY FROM OBSERVER.
A WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM REACHED 34 KNOTS OR 39 MPH AND WAS MEASURED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT SMITH SHOAL LIGHT.

AN ON-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED SUCCESSIVE WATERSPOUTS ALONG A CLOUD LINE ORIENTED EAST TO WEST JUST NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE LONGEST DURATION WAS 11 MINUTES, WITH THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL EXTENDING APPROXIMATELY ONE THIRD OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. VIEW OF THE SEA SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 1 TO 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED THREE-FOURTHS OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON, BUT THE SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTIONS. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE WEST-SOUTHWEST. AT ONE POINT THE WATERSPOUT WAS NEARLY VERTICAL BEFORE TILTING AT A 45 DEGREE ANGLE AND DISSIPATING AT 811 PM. DURATION APPROXIMATELY 15 MINUTES.

MONROE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF UPPER SUGARLOAF KEY. OBSERVED FROM RAMROD KEY LOOKING WEST TOWARD CUDJOE KEY. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED HALFWAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. THE SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTIONS. DURATION UNKNOWN, BUT IT WAS OBSERVED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A VERY THIN WATERSPOUT NORTH OF STOCK ISLAND AND IT WAS ALSO OBSERVED FROM THE NWS OFFICE IN KEY WEST. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED APPROXIMATELY THREE-QUARTERS OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. THE SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTIONS. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 9 MINUTES.

SEVERAL WATERSPOUTS WERE REPORTED NORTH OF KEY WEST AND STOCK ISLAND BY MULTIPLE SOURCES. PICTURE ALSO SUBMITTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF ONE NORTH OF KEY WEST. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE FOR ONE OF THE WATERSPOUTS, WITH A CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. ADDITIONAL CONDENSATION CLOUDS DID NOT FULLY EXTEND FROM CLOUD BASE TO SURFACE. WATERSPOUTS DISSIPATED BY 215 PM EDT, DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 11 MINUTES.
